event: 63 years old
female
patient
diagnosed with papillary thyroid carcinoma
treated by resection and radiotherapy
developed a stenosis of the esophagus
repeated aspiration
several episodes of respiratory insufficiency due to pneumonia and purulent pleurisy
treated by pleurectomy
developed a restrictive ventilation pattern
developed a recurrent nerve palsy
treated by percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy and tracheostoma
put on home ventilation
decreased mobility
never left the bed or nursing chair
secondary depression
nearly stopped talking
mandible was nearly fixed
diagnosed with stenosis of the left axis vertebralis
developed furthermore stenosis of the internal axis carotis on both sides
diagnosed with arterial hypertension
diagnosed with secondary lactase deficiency
esophageal stenosis was dilated
referred to the University of Erlangen
diagnosed with a fistula between the esophagus and tracheal membrane
suffered from pneumonia by 4-multiresistente gramnegative Pseudomonas aeruginosa in the right lung
put on veno-venous extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (vv-ECMO)
partial thromboplastin time of 60 seconds in a preseptic status
ventilated through a tracheostoma
tracheal cannula ended shortly beneath the lower limit of the mediastinal fistula
decision to try endobronchial stenting
stenting was performed
vv-ECMO began to be partly ineffective
high volume input of physiological saline was needed
retrograde stenting was performed
the manually compressed “y” of the FS was pushed downward on the main carina
the frontal surface of the stent was cut with at least 1 cm opening in the longitudinal axis
the lower new edge of this stoma was fixed subcutaneously
a jagwire was introduced through the mouth into the trachea
this above-mentioned soft-tip stiff DLET was introduced for more stability
the distal jagwire was introduced into the oral orifice of the FS
the oral part of the FS was still outside the body
the FS flipped with its upper part over the soft-tip stiff DLET into the upper third of the trachea
the whole fistula was bridged by the FS up to the level of the vocal cords
the upper edge of the new FS stoma was fixed subcutaneously
a regular tracheal cannula was introduced for ventilation
the lungs were not aerated
spontaneous breathing work increased
vv-ECMO support was reduced
the lungs became re-aerated again
the patient woke up again
could communicate with her family by writing and her eyes
infections continued to be very severe
spontaneous work of breathing never exceeded a tidal ventilation of 170 mL per breath
reduction of intravenous saline injection was limited
actively decided to reduce the vv-ECMO support
died on 18 November 2016 due to pulmonary infection
